footer.copy: Promoting Skills for Success Cambodia. All rights reserved.

hero.title: Empowering Cambodian Youth through Skills and Education
hero.subtitle: Promoting Skills for Success (PSS) is a local NGO registered with Cambodia’s Ministry of Interior. It fights poverty by unlocking the potential of disadvantaged youth through access to digital education.

about.title: Fighting Poverty through Digital Education
about.subtitle: PSS is the local operational partner of Passerelles Numériques Cambodia (PNC), which began in 2005 in Phnom Penh and offered a two-year, full-time IT training program for underprivileged youth. The program combines software development with soft skills, English, wellbeing activities, and strong employability support. PSS also provides technical support for NomadLab, an open-source solution for ICT infrastructure used as an educational tool for youth clubs, students, teachers, and prisoners in Cambodia to help expand digital access and build digital skills.

partner.title: Our Partners
partner.subtitle: We are proud to collaborate with a diverse range of partners who share our mission of empowering youth through skills and digital education.

impact.description: At PSS in partnership with PNC and others, we believe real impact is measured in real outcomes, skills gained, futures transformed, and communities strengthened. Our program empowers young people especially those from rural and disadvantaged backgrounds with the technical expertise and professional support needed to thrive in the digital economy.
impact.quote: "With complete support for accommodation, food, equipment, and fully covered health costs, students can focus on learning and building a stable future."
impact.stat1_sub: Since 2005 following PNC's 20 years of experience
impact.stat2_sub: of graduates work in the IT sector
impact.stat3_sub: find a job within 2 months of graduation
impact.stat4_sub: At least 50% of our students are girls. All of them are underprivileged youths including ethnic minorities

context.title: Challenges Youth Face
context.s1_label: of Cambodia’s population lives in rural and remote areas
context.s2_label: of the population lives on less than USD 3 per day
context.s3_label: higher education enrollment rate in Cambodia
context.s4_label: of higher education enrolment is in STEM programs. Men are 2.5 times more likely than women to choose STEM

hero.subtitle: PSS is the local operational partner of Passerelles Numériques Cambodia (PNC), which began in 2005 in Phnom Penh and offered a two-year, full-time IT training program for underprivileged youth. The program combines software development with soft skills, English, wellbeing activities, and strong employability support. PSS also provides technical support for NomadLab, an open-source solution for ICT infrastructure used as an educational tool for youth clubs, students, teachers, and prisoners in Cambodia to help expand digital access and build digital skills.

mission_vision.mission_desc: To provide high-quality IT training, professional skills, and employment support to underprivileged Cambodian youth, enabling them to access quality employment and build sustainable livelihoods.
mission_vision.mission_point1: Encourage and motivate youth to continue their education
mission_vision.mission_point2: Provide opportunities for youth to pursue higher education and specialization in Information Technology (IT)
mission_vision.mission_point3: Contribute to the Royal Government’s efforts to build and develop human capital
mission_vision.mission_point4: Support the delivery of skills training and foreign language training
mission_vision.vision_desc: PSS envisions a Cambodia where all youth, regardless of background, have access to quality education and the opportunities to fulfil their potential in the digital economy.

director_message.message_line1: I am honored to join Promoting Skills for Success (PSS) as the Executive Director. Today in Cambodia, only about 1 in 3 young people have a job that matches their education. For young women, the struggle is even harder. Between the lasting effects of the Covid-19 pandemic, global economic and the lack of equal opportunities, too many are being left behind.
director_message.message_line2: At PSS, we believe digital education is the ultimate equalizer and a gateway for a better life. Our programs are built on excellence, combining high-level IT training with the real-world skills that modern employers demand. By maintaining the highest standards in our classrooms, we ensure our graduates don't just find jobs—they become leaders in their fields.
director_message.message_line3: I look forward to collaborating with all our stakeholders, including local partners and communities, to grow our impact. Your support allows us to provide the quality training necessary to transform lives, ensuring every young woman in Cambodia has the opportunity to thrive in the digital age. Let’s build this future together!

core_values.eye_catching_title: We strive to employ and pass on these values to our beneficiaries
core_values.title: Our Core Values
core_values.value1_desc: We build trust through open communication and reliability, ensuring that our students and stakeholders feel secure in their relationships with us
core_values.value2_desc: We instill a sense of responsibility in our students, encouraging them to take ownership of their learning and future, while also being accountable to their community
core_values.value3_desc: We promote solidarity by fostering a sense of belonging and support among our students, encouraging them to uplift one another and collaborate towards common goals
core_values.value4_desc: We create an environment of mutual respect, valuing each individual's unique background and contributions, which enhances the learning experience and community spirit.

journey.title: Our Evolution Since 2005
journey.event1: Center for Information System Training (CIST) and Passerelles Numériques Cambodia founded; launches its first 2-year IT training program with 25 students.
journey.event2: Runs a 6-month Data Management Program, reaching more than 400 graduates, who pursued further studies and work with Digital Divide Data.
journey.event3: Khmer Typing Tool (National Digital Learning Platform): A proven, long-running national digital product supporting Khmer literacy and digital skills
journey.event4: NomadLab using tablets and computer-tracking & deployment management platform: An open-source solution for ICT infrastructure used as an educational tool for youth clubs, students, teachers, and prisoners in Cambodia to help expand digital access and build digital skills.
journey.event5: Skill Development Program (SDP), Education in Prison which is an offline e-learning platform to support prisoners to access and build digital skills.
journey.event6_title: PSS Cambodia Becomes a Local NGO
journey.event6: PSS Cambodia becomes a local NGO, registered with the Ministry of Social Affairs, Labour and Employment.

policies.title: Our Policies & Commitment
policies.subtitle: We are dedicated to the highest standards of ethical practice, safety, and absolute transparency. Our core policies ensure the protection of the communities we serve, our staff, and the generous supporters who make our mission possible.
policies.policy1_desc: Our Child Safety policy at PSS prioritizes the protection and well-being of youths in all aspects of our operation. We enforce strict guidelines to ensure a safe environment, aligned with international standards and best practices....
policies.policy2_desc: We are committed to promote the well-being of our employees by creating a safe, inclusive and supportive work environment. This includes not only implementing strict workplace safety policies but also promoting an organizational culture that values the physical and mental health of employees.
policies.policy3_title: Global Anti-Fraud, Anti-Bribery and Corruption Policy
policies.policy3_desc: Our global anti-fraud, anti-bribery and corruption policy at PSS ensures stringent measures are in place to prevent unethical practices. We enforce strict guidelines and conduct regular audits to uphold integrity across all operations and engagements.
policies.policy4_title: Donor Safety, Donation Acceptance & Privacy Policy
policies.policy4_desc: Our donor safety, donation acceptance & privacy policy ensures the secure and ethical handling of financial transactions and donor information. We adhere to strict protocols to ensure transparency, protect donor privacy, and maintain the trust of our partners and stakeholders.

hero.subtitle: Our team combines decades of experience in education, technology, and social impact to ensure every student has the support they need to succeed.

executive_team.title: Meet Our Executive Team
executive_team.selection_director_role: Director of Student Selection and Career Guidance

board.title: Our Board of Directors
board.desc: Providing strategic oversight and ensuring the long-term sustainability of our mission.

hero.subtitle: Built on 20 years of PNC experience, the program is aligned with market needs and combines IT training with professional development, soft skills, English, wellbeing activities, and strong employability support. It helps motivated students progress from zero to job-ready.

majors.major1_desc: The graduate will have strong front- and back-end skills, database management, and deployment capabilities. They are ready to step into roles as Web Programmers, Quality Assurance, Junior Data Analytics, database administrator etc.
majors.major2_desc: The graduates will possess strong technical and hands-on skills in IT support, networking, system administration, and cybersecurity. Their potential job roles include System Administrator, Network Engineer, Cloud Administrator, Cybersecurity Analyst,  DevOps engineer, IT Support Specialist etc.

subject_support.english_pl_desc: Technical skills open the door, but professional skills build the career. We integrate language training and soft-skill workshops daily to ensure students are ready for international workplace standards.
subject_support.english_bage_desc: Business communication and technical English for IT documentation.
subject_support.pl_bage_desc: Critical thinking, problem-solving, and emotional intelligence.
subject_support.metoring_desc: Every student is paired with a mentor, providing guidance on career paths, interview preparation, and workplace etiquette.
subject_support.employment_desc: From CV workshops to mock interviews, our team provides ongoing support until the student secures their first quality job.

selection.process_desc: Every year from Jan to Oct, we select 75-100 youth from disadvantaged backgrounds who are motivated to bridge the digital gap.
selection.target_label: Target for Female Students in Tech

life_support.title: Life Skills & Wellbeing
life_support.extra_course_desc: Personal development through Music, Sports, and specialized clubs like Photography and Reading.
life_support.subsistence_desc: Secure accommodation, healthy meals, and a monthly allowance to cover all student needs.
life_support.health_desc: Coverage for health costs and socio-educational guidance from our team of dedicated educators.

join_us.title: Join our community
join_us.desc: Whether as a student, mentor, or partner, there's a place for you.

stories.title: Stories that show what’s possible
stories.subtitle: Built on 20 years of PNC experience:
stories.desc_1: Every cohort brings new momentum. From students who begin with little to no experience, to graduates building real careers in web and software development—our impact is reflected in the daily progress of individuals and the long-term benefits for their families.
stories.desc_2_line_1: Discover firsthand testimonies and updates from across PNC:
stories.desc_2_line_2: training achievements, graduation moments, and milestones that demonstrate how education and opportunity create lasting change.

alumini_1.title: A Second Chance at Life
alumini_1.name: Nang Chhitchhanut
alumini_1.intro: At just 17 years old, Nang Chhitchhanut from Battambang Province has already experienced more struggle than most. Her family fell deep into debt, and constant arguments at home made it difficult for her to focus on school.
alumini_1.quote_1: I often felt hopeless. I didn’t think I could study further after high school. We had no money, and my grades were not good.
alumini_1.body_1: Her life began to change when she attended an information session by Passerelles Numériques Cambodia (PNC). Learning about the scholarship program opened a small window of hope. At first, she hesitated as she was afraid it might be too good to be true. But when she discovered that several students from her own school had received the same scholarship and were now working in good jobs, both she and her family felt confident that PNC could offer her a brighter future.
alumini_1.quote_2: PNC didn’t just give me education; it gave me confidence, discipline, and purpose.
alumini_1.body_2: At PNC, Chhitchhanut found herself in a completely different learning environment. She realized that her poor high school grades did not define her potential. The teachers supported her every step of the way, not just with technical lessons but with life skills and confidence-building. For her family, the scholarship meant relief. Coming from a poor household, PNC’s full support eased their worries. Now her parents no longer have to struggle to pay for her schooling and can focus on her younger siblings.
alumini_1.impact: Through SDF’s support, Chhitchhanut can study without financial hardship. She dreams of becoming a full-stack developer and is already eyeing companies for her internship next year.
alumini_1.closing_quote: SDF and PNC changed my life. Without them, I would still be trapped in fear and poverty. Now, I can see my future clearly.

alumini_2.title: Becoming the Man of His Own Future
alumini_2.name: Nea Piset
alumini_2.intro: Nea Piset lost his father when he was only six years old. Raised by a single mother who struggled to provide, Piset’s early life was filled with uncertainty.
alumini_2.quote_1: Sometimes I thought I should stop studying and just find work to help my mom.
alumini_2.body_1: Fate took a turn when he was introduced to PNC and the scholarship program supported by the Skills Development Fund (SDF). Moving to the city was a challenge; he was scared, knew no one, and lacked confidence in speaking.
alumini_2.quote_2: The teachers always motivated us. They didn’t just teach us lessons; they guided us in life.
alumini_2.body_2: PNC’s unique approach helped him open up. Once shy and quiet, Piset became a leader among his classmates—proactive, well-mannered, and dependable. He now takes every opportunity to build relationships with alumni and connect with companies during career events.
alumini_2.impact: He has already started exploring potential internship placements for his second year, aiming to put his IT knowledge into practice.
alumini_2.closing_quote: Without PNC and SDF, there would be no today’s Piset. They didn’t just give me education; they gave me a chance to believe in myself.

alumini_3.name: Poeun Sokunthea
alumini_3.qoute_1: PNC means growth, opportunity, and preparation for my future career. It has given me the technical knowledge and practical skills needed to succeed in the IT industry.The supportive learning environment, hands-on projects, and professional development activities have helped me build confidence and adapt to real-worldchallenges. Beyond academics, PNC has encouraged me to think critically,collaborate effectively, and communicate clearly—skills that are essential in anycareer. The mentoring from my instructors and the teamwork with my classmateshave pushed me to the limit and constantly improved.
alumini_3.qoute_2: Most importantly, PNC has defined not only my career path, but also my mindset. I now see challenges as opportunities to grow. I am proud to be part of a community that believes in empowering students and making a lasting impact on them.
alumini_3.qoute_3: Thank you so much for supporting me and giving me this part of my life.

partner.name: Peng Chanmony
partner.qoute_1: On behalf of Daiichi Life, we proudly acknowledge our partnership with PNC in developing  future talent. Over the past three consecutive years, Daiichi Life has provided internship opportunities to PNC students, enabling them to gain valuable industry exposure and practical skills.
partner.qoute_2: This initiative reflects our commitment to corporate social responsibility and talent development. We are pleased to see PNC students grow through this program, with some progressing into full-time roles.
partner.qoute_3: We look forward to continuing this meaningful collaboration.

path_of_donation.title: Where Your Donation Goes
path_of_donation.subtitle: Your support directly impacts our students' lives. Here's how your contribution is allocated:
path_of_donation.total_budget: The total cost for one student is roughly $2,500 per year, covering everything from professional IT instruction to their daily three meals and healthcare.
